title: Timely harvesting
summary: Harvest as soon as grain is mature to reduce field exposure to moisture, pests, and mould spores.

title: Sort and remove damaged grain
summary: Remove pest-damaged, shrivelled, or discoloured grain before it enters the drying or storage process.

title: Dry grain promptly
summary: Begin drying within 12 hours of harvest, especially if grain was harvested damp. Sun-dry on clean tarps until moisture is safe.

title: Use clean harvesting surfaces
summary: Keep tarps and equipment clean and free of soil and prior crop debris before each use.

title: Dry on clean surfaces
summary: Use a clean tarp, mat, or concrete floor for drying. Avoid bare soil to prevent moisture and spore pickup.

title: Stir grain during drying
summary: Stir grain often during sun-drying to ensure even drying and prevent hidden damp pockets.

title: Dry before processing
summary: Ensure grain is fully dry before shelling, milling, or other processing to prevent kernel damage and mould entry.

title: Clean processing equipment between batches
summary: Remove debris and residue from shellers, mills, and containers between batches to prevent cross-contamination.

title: Keep storage dry and ventilated
summary: Repair roof leaks, improve airflow, and keep storage areas dry. Check stored grain moisture often.

title: Remove visibly mouldy grain from food and feed chains
summary: Discard mouldy, discoloured, or shrivelled grain. Do not use it for human food or animal feed.

title: Keep grain off floors and walls in storage
summary: Use pallets, raised platforms, or racks to prevent direct contact with moisture-prone surfaces.

title: Cover grain during transport
summary: Use tarps or covers to protect grain from rain and moisture during transit.

title: Clean transport between loads
summary: Remove debris and sweep vehicles between trips to avoid cross-contamination from previous cargo.

title: Ensure market storage is dry and ventilated
summary: Market and trader storage should be raised off the ground, covered, and well-ventilated to slow mould spread.

title: Reject mouldy grain at intake
summary: Establish a clear visual inspection routine. Reject visibly damaged, mouldy, or discoloured grain from entering the batch.

title: Segregate grain by quality
summary: Store known-quality grain separately from unknown or lower-grade grain to limit contamination spread.

title: Separate feed and food grain storage
summary: Keep livestock feed and grain for human consumption in separate areas to prevent cross-contamination.

title: Do not feed mouldy grain to livestock
summary: Mouldy grain in feed can introduce aflatoxin into the milk chain. Use only clean, dry feed for dairy animals.

title: Use clean feed for dairy animals
summary: Aflatoxin from feed passes into milk. Source feed from trusted suppliers and store it dry and clean.

title: Share aflatoxin awareness across the chain
summary: Discuss the grain-feed-milk link with family, cooperative members, and neighbours. Shared awareness strengthens prevention.
